step_by_step_thinking:
Answer:

We are looking for what happens if phosphate-bonded investment contains carbon. In phosphate-bonded investments, any organic material (like carbon) can cause decomposition or undesirable reactions during melting. When molten metal comes into contact with carbon-containing materials, it leads to oxidation and formation of black oxides on the surface — commonly seen as "black castings." This is due to reaction between carbon and oxygen from the melt, producing CO/CO₂ gases that form dark deposits.
raw_response:
Answer:

We are looking for what happens if phosphate-bonded investment contains carbon. In phosphate-bonded investments, any organic material (like carbon) can cause decomposition or undesirable reactions during melting. When molten metal comes into contact with carbon-containing materials, it leads to oxidation and formation of black oxides on the surface — commonly seen as "black castings." This is due to reaction between carbon and oxygen from the melt, producing CO/CO₂ gases that form dark deposits.

Answer: B: Black castings
